growth_records.medium: Brackish carbonate-buffered sulfate-reducer medium (Widdel & Bak 1992, without sulfate) + North Sea crude oil

up.method: hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis substrate; specific uptake rate not reported

sec.method: total microcosm CH4 production measured (max 33.8 nmol/h from 10 mM acetate whole community) but not per-organism specific rate

yields.note: range from assuming methanogen contribution proportional to relative abundance

growth_records.cite: Gray et al. The quantitative significance of Syntrophaceae and syntrophic partnerships in methanogenic degradation of crude oil alkanes. Environmental Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.1111/j.1462-2920.2011.02570.x. [PMC3258425]
growth_records.snippet: Calculated specific growth rates were 0.02, 0.01 and 0.01 day-1... Doubling times for Methanomicrobiales were 34 days... growth yields... 0.009-0.036... 0.141-0.565 g(cell-C)/mol(CH4)
growth_records.notes: Environmental microcosm, mixed community. mu = 0.02 day-1 (qPCR exponential fit R2=0.92) = 0.02/24 = 8.3e-4 /h; doubling time reported 34 days = 34*24 = 816 h (note ln2/8.3e-4 = 835 h ~ consistent). Growth yields reported as ranges: 0.009-0.036 g cell-C/g alkane-C and 0.141-0.565 g cell-C/mol CH4. Total methanogen (3 groups) yield 0.011-0.038 g cell-C/g alkane-C, 0.17-0.6 g cell-C/mol CH4. Max total CH4 production from acetate 33.8+/-0.36 nmol/h at 10 mM 2-13C acetate (whole community). Temperature/pH not stated.
